Learn about DynamoDB table design and ma: Learn about DynamoDB table design and management in MBC CQRS Serverless.
MBC CQRS Serverless uses DynamoDB as its: MBC CQRS Serverless uses DynamoDB as its primary data store, implementing CQRS and Event Sourcing patterns through a structured table design. Understanding the table structure is essential for building efficient applications.
In the MBC CQRS Serverless, DynamoDB tab: In the MBC CQRS Serverless, DynamoDB tables are organized into the following types:
Stores all versions for event sourcing: Stores all versions for event sourcing
Stores information about long-running as: Stores information about long-running asynchronous tasks
Holds sequence data for ID generation: Holds sequence data for ID generation
Stores temporary data for import operati: Stores temporary data for import operations via Step Functions
Table definitions are stored in the `pri: Table definitions are stored in the `prisma/dynamodbs` folder. To add a new entity table:
System tables (`tasks`, `sequences`, `im: System tables (`tasks`, `sequences`, `import_tmp`, `session`) have their own JSON definition files in the `prisma/dynamodbs/` folder. These are automatically created during migration:
Temporary import data with DynamoDB Stre: Temporary import data with DynamoDB Streams for [ImportModule](/docs/import)
The `import_tmp.json` template was added: The `import_tmp.json` template was added in [version 1.1.1](/docs/changelog#v111). If you created your project with an earlier version and use the ImportModule, you need to add this file manually. See [Common Issues](/docs/common-issues#missing-import-tmp-table) for details.
All entity tables use a composite primar: All entity tables use a composite primary key. The DATA table and COMMAND table use the same `pk` format but differ in `sk`:
The COMMAND table sort key includes an `: The COMMAND table sort key includes an `@{version}` suffix appended by the framework. Use `removeSortKeyVersion(sk)` (imported from `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/core`) to strip it when querying the DATA table.
All entity tables share these common att: All entity tables share these common attributes:
The default table configuration does not: The default table configuration does not include GSIs. You can add them based on your query patterns. A common pattern is adding a code-index for fast lookups by business code:
Example GSI definition (add to your tabl: Example GSI definition (add to your table configuration):
Find entity by code (requires code-index: Find entity by code (requires code-index GSI)
Always use partition key in queries: Always use partition key in queries
For read-heavy workloads requiring micro: For read-heavy workloads requiring microsecond latency
The framework includes DynamoDB Local fo: The framework includes DynamoDB Local for development:
The actual table names are prefixed with: The actual table names are prefixed with the environment and application name: `{NODE_ENV}-{APP_NAME}-{entity}-{type}` (for example `dev-myapp-order-command`). The names above are the logical suffixes used by `CommandModule.register({ tableName })`.
When to use DynamoDB vs RDS: When to use DynamoDB vs RDS
